text:
Once upon a time in the quaint town of Little Whisperer lived a young girl named Lily. Lily found solace in art. Each day after school, she would sketch landscapes or flowers with a sense of magic that seemed to infuse her drawings.

One rainy afternoon, as she stayed indoors crafting her latest masterpiece, the doorbell chimed. It was the mayor of Little Whisperer. With a puffed-up chest and a smile so bright it seemed to scatter sunlight, he told Lily that a mysterious figure wanted to see her art. He'd heard her artwork held enchantment.

Curious and ever kind, Lily ran to the door. There stood an old man with a cloak as dark as the forests around Whisperer. His beard was as white as snow and eyes as deep and quiet as the sea at night.

"We're making a collector," he said in a voice that seemed to soothe the soul. "One who appreciates your magic."
